DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-27-2023 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. DAVID GARIBALDI, Esq. of Peretz & Associates (Tel: 415-732-3777) for plaintiff;
Attys. BRENDAN DOLAN, Esq. and KAYLA MALONE, Esq. of Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton LLP (Tel: 415-434-9100) for defendants Oracale America, Inc. and Roland McLeod.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS ROLAND MCLEOD, AND ORACLE AMERICA, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' statute-of-limitations demurrer to the complaint's fifth (wrongful termination in violation of public policy), sixth (intentional infliction of emotional distress) and seventh (negligent infliction of emotional distress) causes of action is sustained without leave to amend.
The parties agree that two years is the applicable limitations period. Terminated from his job in July 2020, plaintiff sued on December 27, 2022 - well past the two-year deadline absent tolling.
Plaintiff relies on Covid Emergency Rule 9: "Notwithstanding any other law, the statutes of limitations and repose for civil causes of action that exceed 180 days are tolled from April 6, 2020 to October 1, 2020." However, October 2, 2020 to December 27, 2022 was more than two years.
Plaintiff argues that he "had two years and 178 days to file his Complaint." (Opp. 5:7.) But Emergency Rule 9 set a specific tolling period (April 6-October 1, 2020); it did not add 178 days to statutes of limitations.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
